source.description: Six-panel contact sheet generated for a pencil-and-colored-pencil backyard pinwheel sequence, then cropped into lesson assets.
source.notes: Created as the July 2, 2026 current Sketcha.day lesson after avoiding July 4 firework/flag overlap.

process_strategy: The frames keep one pinwheel at the same angle while building the hub, blade triangles, stick, folds, pattern accents, and final restrained color in order.

final_elements.name: center hub circle
final_elements.description: A small round button at the crossing point of the pinwheel blades.

final_elements.name: cross guide axes
final_elements.description: Light diagonal guide lines that set the four blade directions.

final_elements.name: four folded blades
final_elements.description: Four curved triangular pinwheel blades wrapped around the center hub.

final_elements.name: slender stick
final_elements.description: A narrow handle descending from the pinwheel center.

final_elements.name: blade fold lines
final_elements.description: Short interior creases that make each paper blade look folded.

final_elements.name: small pattern dots
final_elements.description: Simple decorative dots placed on the paper blades.

final_elements.name: restrained red and blue color
final_elements.description: Light colored-pencil accents on alternating blades, kept transparent enough for graphite lines to show.

final_elements.name: soft ground shadow
final_elements.description: A pale shadow near the lower stick that helps the pinwheel sit on the page.

frames.visible_job: Adds the small center hub circle and light diagonal guide axes for the blade directions.
Builds four curved triangular paper blades around the hub while keeping the same angle.
Refines the outside blade edges and adds a slender handle below the hub.
Adds interior fold lines on the already drawn blades to show the curled paper shape.
Adds small blade dots, restrained red and blue color, and a soft ground shadow.

final_step.visible_job: Only darkens the keeper contours, clarifies the folds, and deepens the existing color and shadow.

rejection_checks: No blade, stick, pattern mark, or color area should first appear in the final image.
No frame may darken, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pinwheel angle, scale, and placement as the step frames.
